name: Pulikacchal
recipeIngredient: Soaked tamarind - about a large lemon size
6 Green chillies, slit into two (or more if it isn't hot)
Ginger, fresh, cut into thin strips (about 2 inches)
recipeInstructions:
1. Squeeze out the pulp from the tamarind and set aside.
2. Warm oil, season with mustard seeds first. When they start to pop, add red chillies, chana dal, hing, and curry leaves. When the dal turns red, add green chillies and fry.
3. Add ginger, tamarind extract, a pinch of turmeric, salt to taste, and jaggery. Simmer on low heat until the raw smell disappears and the sauce has reduced to one-third or thickened yet spoonable.
4. Crush the roasted sesame seeds with a rolling pin on paper or in a spice mill rather coarsely. Add this at the end to the pulikacchal. Mix well so there are no lumps.
